summary: From the presented case, five mutable targets drive distress: intrusive visual replay (C01), verbal rumination (C02), immediate physiological reactivity to evaluative cues (C03), anticipatory fear of humiliation (C04), and a lowered threshold for perceiving criticism (C05). The proposed solutions span psychological (CBT/imagery processing, rumination reduction, cognitive reinterpretation, exposure-based strategies), physiological self-regulation (HRV/biofeedback), and social/environmental supports (social connectedness and safe feedback environments). Each lever is designed as a modifiable predictor (not a symptom label), with explicit links to the target criteria, measurable outcomes, a realistic time horizon, feasibility estimates, safety notes, and a mapping query to guide implementation and evaluation.

solutions.solution_candidate: Imagery Rescripting and Rehearsal Therapy (IRT) to reduce intrusive visual replay of the boss incident; includes controlled exposure to the memory with cognitive reframing and brief mindfulness/focus training to reset connection between memory and emotional response.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Re-encodes trauma-related memory, reducing emotional salience and frequency/intensity of involuntary visual replays through systematic exposure and cognitive reframing.
solutions.measurement: Daily intrusion diary: frequency (0-5 per day) and distress intensity (0-10); optional 0-100% day-time recall proportion.
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns with guided, progressive exposure. Monitor distress; pause if flashbacks worsen; if PTSD symptoms emerge, consider clinician involvement.
solutions.mapping_query: Target C01 with imagery-based exposure and cognitive reframing (IRT) to reduce intrusive replay.

solutions.solution_candidate: Rumination-focused CBT techniques: scheduled worry time, cognitive defusion and thought-tracking, plus behavioral activation to reduce time available for rumination.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Decreases persistent verbal rumination by structuring thought processes, increasing cognitive flexibility, and enhancing engagement in meaningful activities.
solutions.measurement: Ruminative thought diary (percentage of day engaged in event-related thoughts); distress rating during ruminations (0-10); weekly self-report of time spent ruminating.
solutions.safety_notes: Encourage emotional expression; avoid suppression of emotions; monitor for worsening mood; escalate if suicidality risk arises.
solutions.mapping_query: Target C02 with rumination-focused CBT strategies.

solutions.solution_candidate: Biofeedback-assisted autonomic regulation: HRV-coherence training and paced diaphragmatic breathing during evaluative cues; integrate with mindfulness and progressive muscle relaxation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improves autonomic flexibility and dampens visceral reactions (e.g., stomach drop) to feedback by strengthening vagal regulation.
solutions.measurement: HRV coherence index during evaluative tasks; self-reported distress (0-10) and frequency of stomach-related symptoms per day/week.
solutions.safety_notes: Safe with proper instruction; avoid hyperventilation; discontinue if dizziness or faintness occurs.
solutions.mapping_query: Target C03 via physiological regulation training (biofeedback + paced breathing).

solutions.solution_candidate: Graded social evaluative exposure with anticipatory anxiety reduction: plan small, safe feedback interactions (one-on-one mock feedback, then real-time feedback) with coaching and post-event reflection; apply expectancy violation techniques.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Own adequate exposure to evaluative situations reduces anticipatory fear and belief that humiliation is inevitable, updating expectancy through corrective experiences.
solutions.measurement: Prior-to-event anticipatory anxiety (0-10); post-event distress (0-10); avoidance frequency (days/week); success of each graded step.
solutions.safety_notes: Ensure supportive, confidential setting; monitor for excessive stress; involve supervisor or facilitator trained in supportive feedback.
solutions.mapping_query: Target C04 with graded exposure and expectancy violation techniques.

solutions.solution_candidate: Cognitive bias modification and reinterpretation training to recalibrate threat interpretation of neutral or mildly critical feedback; teach rapid labeling of distortions and alternative appraisals.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reduces automatic threat interpretation of feedback by retraining interpretation patterns and enhancing tolerance for ambiguity.
solutions.measurement: Rate of neutral cues interpreted as threatening (0-100%); distress during feedback (0-10); post-training change in threat attribution bias (pre vs post).
solutions.safety_notes: No safety concerns; support with alternative explanations; monitor for overcorrection or avoidance of feedback.
solutions.mapping_query: Target C05 with reinterpretation training to reduce threat perception.

solutions.solution_candidate: Social support and relational resilience program: structured peer feedback groups and mentor check-ins to create a supportive feedback climate; normalize feedback processing and practice constructive responses.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhances perceived safety around feedback, lowers threat salience, and broadens interpretation of evaluative cues through positive social experiences and modeled responses.
solutions.measurement: Perceived social support scale; frequency of supportive feedback encounters; changes in anticipatory fear (0-10) and threat interpretation bias (0-100%).
solutions.safety_notes: Maintain confidentiality; align group norms to avoid judgement; monitor for negative peer dynamics.
solutions.mapping_query: Target C04 and C05 with social support and relational skills training.
